## reference · GOV UK Design Principles

* Start with user needs: Identify user needs through research, analysis, and empathy. Don't make assumptions.
* Do less: Focus on the irreducible core and make reusable, shareable solutions.
* Design with data: Use data to drive decision-making and iterate with user feedback.
* Do the hard work to make it simple: Make complex systems simple to use, not just look simple.
* Iterate. Then iterate again: Release minimum viable products, test, and refine based on feedback.
* This is for everyone: Design inclusively, legibly, and readably for the whole country.
* Understand context: Consider the context in which users interact with services.
* Build digital services, not websites: Focus on meeting user needs, not just building web pages.
* Be consistent, not uniform: Use consistent language and design patterns, but allow for improvement and change.
* Make things open: Share code, designs, ideas, and failures to improve services.
* Minimise environmental impact: Reduce energy, water, and material usage in digital services.

Sources: https://www.gov.uk/guidance/government-design-principles
